sanskrit: बुद्धेर्भेदं धृतेश्चैव गुणतस्त्रिविधं श्रृणु । प्रोच्यमानमशेषेण पृथक्त्वेन धनञ्जय ॥
hindiTranslation: हे धनञ्जय  अब तू गुणोंके अनुसार बुद्धि और धृतिके भी तीन प्रकारके भेद अलगअलगरूपसे सुन, जो कि मेरे द्वारा पूर्णरूपसे कहे जा रहे हैं ॥
englishTranslation: O Dhananjaya, now hear the threefold distinctions of understanding and steadfastness according to the three qualities, fully and separately explained.
essence: Understanding and resolve differ by the quality that shapes them.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Krishna asks Arjuna to listen to the three kinds of understanding and steadfastness, explained separately according to the three qualities.
simpleInsight: Clear seeing begins by sorting what kind of mind is at work.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield, Arjuna still stands torn between action and collapse. Krishna now turns to the inner equipment behind choice itself: understanding and steadfastness. He prepares Arjuna to see how the three qualities shape the mind before explaining each one.
modernRelevance: You are staring at two job offers, one safe and one honest, and your mind keeps changing its story. First notice what kind of understanding is speaking, then decide.
translationLiteral: बुद्धेः (of understanding) / भेदम् (the distinctions) / धृतेः (of steadfastness) / च (and) / एव (indeed) / गुणतः (according to the qualities) / त्रिविधम् (threefold) / श्रृणु (hear) / प्रोच्यमानम् (being explained) / अशेषेण (completely) / पृथक्त्वेन (separately) / धनञ्जय (O Dhananjaya)
insight: It shifts attention from the decision itself to the quality of mind making the decision.
